title: satureja vulgaris subsp. villosa
intro: Satureja vulgaris subsp. villosa, also known as woodland savory, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family, native to Europe and western Asia, characterized by its hairy leaves and small, white or pink flowers. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for treating wounds, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The health benefits of woodland savory include its ability to relieve colds, fever, and sore throats, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve circulation. Woodland savory can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
